audit_items.proof_trace: At line 800-803, `nfp_cpp_area_alloc()` succeeds and returns a non-NULL `area`. At line 805-807, `kzalloc(sizeof(*cache), GFP_KERNEL)` can fail under memory pressure, causing the function to return `-ENOMEM` without calling `nfp_cpp_area_free(area)`. The sister function `nfp_cpp_area_alloc_acquire()` (L385-394) demonstrates the correct pattern — it calls `nfp_cpp_area_free(area)` on error — confirming the omission here is not by design.
audit_items.attack_scenario: When the NFP PCI driver probes a device under OOM conditions, `nfp6000_init()` calls `nfp_cpp_area_cache_add()` three times; if `kzalloc` fails on any call after `nfp_cpp_area_alloc()` succeeds, the allocated area object leaks from kernel heap for the device lifetime, contributing to kernel memory exhaustion.
